arts.ttl: Enzo Fernandez sets new Chelsea target as his future becomes clear
Victor Osimhen to Chelsea: Why Maresca MUST sign Nigeria superstar
arts.sum: In addition to the on-loan Galatasaray star, Chelsea are also tracking the progress of Ipswich Town forward Liam Delap and while the England U21 international would be a great signing, the Blues may need to prioritise a deal for Osimhen if they want to challenge for the Premier League title next year. Victor Osimhen is expected to leave Napoli on a permanent deal this summer and Chelsea are primed to rekindle their interest in the Nigeria superstar.

arts.ttl: Exclusive Chelsea transfer news: Victor Osimhen wants Stamford Bridge move
arts.sum: However, Chelsea are determined to sign a new centre forward during the 2025 summer transfer window and the west London giants are ready to rekindle their interest in Osimhen, FootballTransfers has been told. We have been informed that Osimhen has set his sights on completing a move to Stamford Bridge this summer, with the Blues interested in signing the 26-year-old as they look to purchase a prolific goalscorer ahead of the 2025-26 campaign.

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer reveals his 'superpower' after the Chelsea star's stunning rise - as he admits 'people are different'
arts.sum: Palmer, 22, is in the midst of another impressive season, with the attacking midfielder having scored 14 goals and added seven assists in 29 Premier League games.

arts.ttl: Four winners and two losers from Chelsea's Premier League win over Tottenham - opinion
arts.sum: Chelsea made the perfect start after the international break with a clean sheet and three points against Tottenham. The Blues secured a well-deserved 1-0 victory...

arts.ttl: Kevin De Bruyne to Chelsea transfer truth as Man City told shock Premier League return stance
arts.sum: De Bruyne joined Chelsea from KRC Genk in January 2012 before returning to his boyhood club on loan for the remainder of the season . He won six Premier League titles, five League Cups, three Community Shields, two FA Cups, the Champions League and the Club World Cup. De Bruyne will go down as one of the greatest players to play in the English top flight – but his career could have taken a different route had he stayed at Stamford Bridge .
